title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nevadensis
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Rocky Mountain Columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is a perennial plant with delicate, bell-shaped flowers in shades of blue and purple that contain alkaloids and other compounds with potential medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal benefits, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
